content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 14, 1990


Roy W. Lockwood
327 South C Street
Oxnard, CA 93030

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-456

Dear Mr. Lockwood:

You have requested advice under the contribution limitations
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). Because your
request is a general inquiry, we are treating your request as one
for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (copy
enclosed).2

QUESTION

What limitations are there on a candidate's ability to loan
or donate personal funds to his or her own campaign?

CONCLUSION

Contributions and loans from a candidate's personal funds to
his or her own campaign are not limited by the campaign
contributions of the Act. However, all of the candidate's
personal funds that are contributed or loaned to his or her campaign
must be deposited in, and expended from, the campaign bank account
specified on the candidate's campaign bank account statement (Form
502).

FACTS

You are currently contemplating campaigning for a seat on the
Oxnard City Council and also for a seat on the Oxnard Union High

1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-90-456
Page 2


School Board of Trustees. It is your intention to finance each of
the campaigns totally from personal funds.

ANALYSIS

Section 85301(b) provides that the campaign contribution
limits of the Act do not apply to money contributed or loaned by a
candidate from personal funds to his or her own campaign.
However, any personal funds used to promote the election of the
candidate must be deposited in a campaign bank account prior to
the expenditure. (Section 85201(d).) Moreover, all campaign
expenditures must be made from the campaign account. (Section
85201(e).)

Before any contributions may be solicited, received or
deposited in the campaign bank account, including your own
contributions, a candidate must file a candidate intention
statement (Form 501) declaring his or her intent to be a candidate
for a specific office. (Section 85200.) Upon the filing of the
candidate intention statement, the candidate is required to
establish one campaign bank account in an office of a financial
institution located in the state and file a campaign bank account
statement (Form 502). (Section 85201(a).)[superscript 3]

Section 85202(b) provides:

All contributions deposited into the campaign
account shall be deemed to be held in trust for
expenses associated with the election of the
candidate to the specific office for which the
candidate has stated, pursuant to Section 85200,
that he or she intends to seek or expenses
associated with holding that office. (emphasis
added.)

Thus, contributions from your personal funds to your own
campaign are not limited by the campaign contribution limits of
the Act. However, the funds must be deposited in your campaign
bank account before they are spent. Should you decide to run for
both the city council and the high school board, you are required
to establish a separate campaign account for each office for which
you are campaigning prior to soliciting contributions (even your
own). Pursuant to the terms of a currently effective federal


_________________________

3 If you receive no contributions from others, and the only
expenditure of personal funds is to pay your filing fees, you are
not required to open a campaign bank account. (Regulation 18522,
copy enclosed.)

Our File No. I-90-456
Page 3


injunction,4 you are permitted to transfer funds between your
campaign accounts.

If you have any further questions concerning this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                   By:  Deanne Stone
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

SH:DS:aa

Enclosures




_________________________
4    Service Employees International Union, et al. v. Fair Political
Practices Commission (U.S. District Court, Eastern District of
California, Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM). This is subject to
change depending on the terms of the final decision in the SEIU
Case.

sections.analysis:
Section 85301(b) provides that the campaign contribution limits of the Act do not apply to money contributed or loaned by a candidate from personal funds to his or her own campaign. However, any personal funds used to promote the election of the candidate must be deposited in a campaign bank account prior to the expenditure. (Section 85201(d).) Moreover, all campaign expenditures must be made from the campaign account. (Section 85201(e).)

Before any contributions may be solicited, received or deposited in the campaign bank account, including your own contributions, a candidate must file a candidate intention statement (Form 501) declaring his or her intent to be a candidate for a specific office. (Section 85200.) Upon the filing of the candidate intention statement, the candidate is required to establish one campaign bank account at an office of a financial institution located in the state and file a campaign bank account statement (Form 502). (Section 85201(a).)3

Section 85202(b) provides:

All contributions deposited into the campaign account shall be deemed to be held in trust for expenses associated with the election of the candidate to the specific office for which the candidate has stated, pursuant to Section 85200, that he or she intends to seek or expenses associated with holding that office. (emphasis added.)

Thus, contributions from your personal funds to your own campaign are not limited by the campaign contribution limits of the Act. However, the funds must be deposited in your campaign bank account before they are spent. Should you decide to run for both the city council and the high school board, you are required to establish a separate campaign account for each office for which you are campaigning prior to soliciting contributions (even your own). Pursuant to the terms of a currently effective federal

3 If you receive no contributions from others, and the only expenditure of personal funds is to pay your filing fees, you are not required to open a campaign bank account. (Regulation 18522, copy enclosed.)

injunction,4 you are permitted to transfer funds between your campaign accounts.

However, all of the candidate’s personal funds that are donated or loaned to his or her campaign must be deposited in, and expended from, the campaign bank account specified on the candidate’s campaign bank account statement (Form 502).
